title: Corethrogyne tomentella
taxonomy: Corethrogyne tomentella is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is classified as a shrub or subshrub. Corethrogyne tomentella is further classified as a member of the genus Corethrogyne. It is closely related to other plants within the tribe Madinae.
uses: Corethrogyne tomentella, also known as woolly marigold, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help treat wounds and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that the plant's extracts may also have antifungal and antiviral effects. Additionally, the plant's roots are believed to have expectorant properties, which could aid in relieving respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Corethrogyne tomentella for medicinal purposes.

properties: Corethrogyne tomentella, also known as woolly marigold, has been found to possess various therapeutic properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant's roots and leaves contain bioactive molecules that may exhibit antioxidant and analgesic effects, contributing to its potential therapeutic benefits. Additionally, Corethrogyne tomentella has been studied for its potential in treating wounds and infections due to its ability to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic properties and its potential applications in medicine.
constituents: Corethrogyne tomentella is a shrub native to the southwestern United States, and research has identified its active constituents.  The plant's leaves contain flavonoids, such as quercetin, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.  Additionally, Corethrogyne tomentella has been found to contain sesquiterpene lactones, specifically corethrolides and tomentellin, which may have anti-inflammatory effects.  The plant's roots also contain saponins, which could have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.  Further studies are needed to fully understand the active constituents and potential uses of Corethrogyne tomentella.

intro: Corethrogyne tomentella, also known as Corethrogyne, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Corethrogyne tomentella include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Corethrogyne is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including colds, sore throats, and digestive issues.
